name: Shoko (Beef and Spinach Stew)
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp cayenne [more or less to taste]
recipeInstructions:
1. Reserve half a cup of juice from the canned tomatoes and discard the rest.
2. Combine the chile, tomatoes (with reserved juice), onions, and green bell pepper in a food processor; process until the vegetables are minced but not pureed.
3. Heat oil in a large, cast-iron pot; sauté the vegetables and beef for 5 minutes over high heat.
4. Add water (or beef broth), sugar, salt, cayenne, and ginger. Cover, lower the heat, and simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
5. Meanwhile, soak spinach in warm water for 15 minutes; rinse thoroughly, separate, rinse again, shred coarsely, and set aside.
6. After 2 hours of stewing, add spinach to the pot and cook over medium heat for 30 minutes until the water is gone and spinach is cooked.
7. Serve Shoko with boiled rice. Also good served with Yam Foofoo.
